title: Torch, CUDA, and driver must align
canonical_summary: GPU acceleration depends on compatible driver, CUDA runtime, PyTorch build, and optional acceleration libraries.
source_priority_rule: Current official install guidance and local diagnostic output outrank old setup notes.
answer_pattern: Explain alignment as a stack, not a single setting.
exactness_guard: Do not claim a specific install command is current without verification.

title: torch.cuda availability is a runtime signal
canonical_summary: `torch.cuda.is_available()` is a useful signal for whether the running Python environment can access GPU support.
source_priority_rule: Live diagnostic output outranks assumed hardware capability.
answer_pattern: Explain that the running environment matters, not just installed hardware.
exactness_guard: Do not assume GPU access because the PC has an NVIDIA card.

title: Optional acceleration libraries add another compatibility layer
canonical_summary: xFormers, flash attention, Triton, or related acceleration packages can fail independently from base PyTorch.
source_priority_rule: Import errors and package versions drive diagnosis.
answer_pattern: Explain that optional speedups can be disabled or matched separately.
exactness_guard: Do not treat an optional acceleration failure as proof the entire GPU stack is broken.

title: Environment isolation affects CUDA visibility
user_phrasing: cuda works in one app not another
comfyui sees cuda but script doesn't
canonical_summary: Different venvs can have different PyTorch builds, so CUDA may work in one app and fail in another.
source_priority_rule: Active interpreter and pip environment evidence determine the answer.
exactness_guard: Do not assume one successful app proves all environments are configured.

title: PyTorch reports CUDA unavailable
canonical_summary: This failure means the active Python environment cannot currently use CUDA through PyTorch.
answer_pattern: Recommend checking active interpreter, torch build, and driver visibility.

title: DLL load or binary mismatch error
canonical_summary: DLL or binary mismatch errors often indicate incompatible package wheels, CUDA runtime expectations, or environment contamination.
source_priority_rule: Exact error text and versions outrank broad advice.
answer_pattern: Identify mismatch class and recommend version inventory before install changes.
exactness_guard: Do not guess package versions without verification.

title: xFormers or attention backend fails
canonical_summary: An attention backend failure may be isolated from core PyTorch CUDA support.
source_priority_rule: Check core CUDA separately from optional backend imports.
answer_pattern: Recommend testing torch.cuda separately and disabling or matching the backend if needed.
exactness_guard: Do not assume xFormers failure means torch CUDA is unavailable.

title: CUDA unavailable vs VRAM OOM
user_phrasing: cuda not available or out of memory
canonical_summary: CUDA unavailable means the environment cannot access GPU support; OOM means it can access the GPU but the job exceeds available memory.
answer_pattern: Separate access failure from memory capacity failure.
exactness_guard: Do not solve CUDA unavailable by lowering batch size.

title: Runtime alignment vs Windows launcher issue
user_phrasing: is this torch or launch path
script doesn't start vs cuda error
canonical_summary: If the script never enters the Python app due to path or permission errors, route to Windows setup. If Python runs and reports torch/CUDA errors, route here.
answer_pattern: Identify whether Python runtime was reached before diagnosing CUDA.
exactness_guard: Do not debug torch if the launcher itself failed.

title: Official/current install docs win for version commands
user_phrasing: what torch command should I use
canonical_summary: For exact install commands and current package versions, official/current PyTorch or package documentation outranks archived notes.
source_priority_rule: Current official docs and live diagnostics outrank memory.
answer_pattern: State that exact commands must be verified before use.
exactness_guard: Do not freeze stale install commands into the answer.

title: Do not confuse CUDA alignment with model folder layout
user_phrasing: gpu not working or model missing
canonical_summary: CUDA alignment concerns whether the runtime can use the GPU. Model-folder layout concerns whether model files are found.
answer_pattern: Separate compute backend failures from file-location failures.
exactness_guard: Do not redownload models to fix torch.cuda errors.

title: How to answer CUDA/Torch alignment questions
user_phrasing: how should assistant answer cuda torch issues
canonical_summary: Answers should gather exact error text, identify active environment, distinguish unavailable vs OOM, and avoid stale install commands unless verified.
answer_pattern: Use: error text -> environment -> branch -> verified next step.
exactness_guard: Do not provide unverified install commands as final authority.
